workContext.summary: User is Adrien (Muhabuki), Founder & CEO of BookiStudios (BUKIMIND), which runs platforms including LoveFlix, Deerflow, and BookiStudios AI.

topOfMind.summary: User is actively building a custom Notion skill within Deerflow/BookiStudios AI to connect to their Notion workspace, using the Notion API and existing web fetch/file tools. This replaces the earlier attempt to set up an MCP server, as the platform does not support MCP. User also has a goal to create a video about a cat running down the street. Additionally, as Founder & CEO of BookiStudios, user oversees LoveFlix, Deerflow, and BookiStudios AI platforms.

recentMonths.summary: User has a goal to create a video about a cat running down the street. In recent conversations, user confirmed tools are active and initiated a request to connect Notion to their Deerflow/Bookistudios account. Initial attempts to set up a custom MCP server failed because the platform does not support MCP connections. User considered two approaches: building a custom Notion skill within Deerflow using existing tools, or building a standalone MCP server. User decided to proceed with Option 1 (building a Notion skill inside Deerflow) and provided a summary of the conversation to guide implementation.

longTermBackground.summary: User is the founder and CEO of BookiStudios (BUKIMIND), overseeing products like LoveFlix, Deerflow, and BookiStudios AI.

facts.content: User wants to create a video about a cat running down the street.
User wants to set up a custom MCP server to connect Notion to their Deerflow/Bookistudios account.
User is Adrien (Muhabuki), Founder & CEO of BookiStudios (BUKIMIND), which includes LoveFlix, Deerflow, and BookiStudios AI.
Deerflow/Bookistudios AI platform does not currently support MCP connections (no MCP server/client config mechanism).
Web search and file tools are fully operational in the Deerflow/Bookistudios AI environment.
User opted to build a custom Notion skill within Deerflow/BookiStudios AI using the Notion API, instead of setting up an MCP server.
